summary.title: Vector Store Data Flow
summary.description: The GenAI Vector Store is a service which takes customer selected data and vectorizes that data through an AI model.  The customer is then able to access the data by submitting queries.  The service receives the query, creates a vector of the query, and compares the request vector to vectorized data.

data.name: Infinity
data.description: The infinity product is required to access the vector store service.  The infinity environment is the primary input location for the VS service.  Data is input by the end users and sent to the service for vectorization.  Any document can be sent for vectorization, there is no control of data sent to the VS service by the end user customer.
data.reasonOutOfScope: External service, not managed by the Vector Store team

threats.title: Fake Infinity Environment
threats.description: A fake infinity environment could access the VS service.
threats.mitigation: Infinity environments are kept within a VPC and SAX authentication is required to validate the user(s) accessing the service

data.name: Vector Store Database
data.description:
DBMS SCE governed by DBMS team.  
Data stored is based on customer requests, data is stored in vectors.  DATA: Customer vectors, customer queries, configuration information, schema information
data.reasonOutOfScope: Database is managed by DBMS team

threats.title: Connection Limit Exceeded
threats.description: Max number of connections to the database could limit new connections
threats.mitigation: There is a queue system in place which is managed in the vector store code.  This system will retry requests that are denied if the maximum number of connections are met against a particular model.

data.name:
Vector Store:
Service
data.description: This is the primary service within the VS service SCE.  The primary functionality of this service is the vectorization of data which is then stored in the VS database. DATA: Customer queries, customer documents

threats.title: Flooding of events
threats.description: It is possible that multiple requests are sent to the service simultaneously causing the service to be unresponsive
threats.mitigation: There is a queue system in place.  If the number of requests exceeds what is possible to request at the model level the requests are queued and sent once tokens are available.

threats.title: Malicious Data
threats.description: Customers could potentially send malicious data either intentionally or unintentionally.
threats.mitigation: Only valid JSON is allowed into the VS service.  Additional formats may be added in the future, but for now the JSON is validated before being sent to the service.

data.name:
Backing Service: 
GenAI Assistant Service
data.description: The autopilot backing service has access to the vector store service.
data.reasonOutOfScope: External service, not managed by the Vector Store team

data.name: Vector Store: Ops
data.description:
the VS ops service is a component of the VS SCE
This component is used for operational tasks such as modifications to schema, sizing changes, or further configuration changes for the VS service. DATA: configuration information, operations requests.

data.name:
Vector Store:
 Background
data.description: The vector store background service is a temporary service being used during the schema update to the vector store.  It's primary function is replication of data from the old to the new schema.

data.name: GOC
data.description: The GOC has the ability to provision and access the vector store services deployed within their access zone.
data.reasonOutOfScope: External service, not managed by the Vector Store team

threats.title: Fake GOC Environment
threats.description: A fake GOC environment could access the VS service
threats.mitigation: GOC uses proper scopes and SAX authentication to verify access to service

data.name: Provisioning Service
data.description: The provisioning service is used to deploy the vector store service
data.reasonOutOfScope: External service, not managed by the Vector Store team

threats.title: Fake Provisioning Service
threats.description: Provisioning service has access to build/modify the VS service and database
threats.mitigation: The provisioning service uses proper scopes and SAX authentication to verify access to service

data.name: Schema sync
data.description: Communication from the VS background to the VS DB.  TCP/SSL(port 5432). DATA: Database schema (temporary while schema update is happening, to be removed after new schema is in place)

data.name:
Storage of vectorized
data into VS DB
data.description: VS service communication to the VS database, TCP/SSL(port 5432).  Data stored is the vectors which are created during processing within VS service. DATA: vectorized customer documents

labels:
Storage of vectorized
data into VS DB

data.name:
Operations requests 
sent to VS DB
data.description: VS Ops communication to VS DB, TCP/SSL(port 5432). Pps service may request data changes.  DATA: operational configuration

labels:
Operations requests 
sent to VS DB

data.name: Validate SAX Token
data.description: Data transmitted is authentication data which validates that requests are from a valid source through OKTA. Data sent over HTTPS.  DATA: SAX token

threats.title: Compromised SAX token
threats.description: It could be possible for a SAX token to be compromised allowing a malicious actor to send calls to the service
threats.mitigation: The SAX token is protected by multiple security measures.  A valid token must come from within the Pega application, only internal services can generate/validate tokens.  Additionally our service is a backend service, so the standard Pega application protections are in place to avoid the token from being compromised. Additionally communication is sent over HTTPS with valid certificates.

data.name:
DB Tools requests
sent to VS DB
data.description: DB Tools communication into the VS database, TCP/SSL(port 5432).  Data transmitted is requests to see/modify database contents.  DATA: DB config changes

labels:
DB Tools requests
sent to VS DB

data.name:
Operations data 
to VS Service
data.description: Communication between GOC and the VS Ops service, https and SAX authentication utilized.  Data is in form of requests to service to make infrastructure adjustments.  DATA: Operation information

threats.title: Communication between Infinity and Service
threats.description: Data transmitted should be using HTTPS.  Any other form of communication may lead to possible tampering of data.
threats.mitigation: Data flowing from infinity to VS OPS service uses https and also requires SAX authentication.

data.name:
Provisioning 
configuration
data.description: Communication between provisioning service and the VS Ops service, https and SAX authentication utilized.  Data contains information about the environment to be provisioned.  DATA: Configuration Information

threats.title: Communication between Infinity and Service
threats.description: Data transmitted should be using HTTPS.  Any other form of communication may lead to possible tampering of data.
threats.mitigation: Data flowing from infinity to VS OPS service uses https and also requires SAX authentication

data.name: Request for DB changes
data.description: The dev & ops teams can send requests to change/update the database through DBTools

threats.title: Intercept commands
threats.description: Commands sent from dev/ops teams could be intercepted in transit and modified by a malicious actor
threats.mitigation: The communication between the Pega employees and the DB tools service utilizes the standard security practices.  LDAP is in place to validate users, and tooling is only accessible through Pega supplied devices.

data.name:
Autopilot data 
into VS Service
data.description: Communication between the Autopilot service and the VS service, https and SAX authentication utilized.  Data is customer selected, VS team has no control over what is sent.  DATA: Customer queries

threats.title: Communication between Infinity and Service
threats.description: Data transmitted should be using HTTPS.  Any other form of communication may lead to possible tampering of data.
threats.mitigation: Data flowing from infinity to VS service uses https and also requires SAX authentication.

data.name:
Vectorized data sent to
SC service for chunking
data.description: Communication between the VS service and smart chunking, https and SAX authentication utilized.  Data is sent to SC service to be chunked before vectorization. DATA: Customer documents

threats.title: Intercepted data
threats.description: Data in transit could be intercepted and changed.
threats.mitigation: Data flowing from VS to smart chunking service uses https and also requires SAX authentication.

threats.title: Weak TLS
threats.description: A weak TLS algorithm could allow for communication between the services to be compromised
threats.mitigation: All communication will be using TLS 1.2 or newer

labels:
Vectorized data sent to
SC service for chunking

data.name:
Chunked data sent
to Gateway for vectorization
data.description: Data flow between Smart Chunking and gateway, https and SAX authentication utilized. SC service processes data into chunks and sends data to GW.  DATA: Customer documents chunked, to be vectorized.
data.reasonOutOfScope: communication between two out of scope items

threats.title: Intercepted data
threats.description: Data in transit could be intercepted and changed.
threats.mitigation: Data flowing from smart chunking to gateway service uses https and also requires SAX authentication.

threats.title: Weak TLS
threats.description: A weak TLS algorithm could allow for communication between the services to be compromised
threats.mitigation: All communication will be using TLS 1.2 or newer

labels:
Chunked data sent
to Gateway for vectorization

data.name: Data into VS Service
data.description: Communication from Infinity to the VS service, https and SAX authentication utilized.  Data is customer selected, VS team has no control over what is sent.  DATA: Customer documents

threats.title: Communication between Infinity and Service
threats.description: Data being sent to the VS service from Infinity may be tampered with by a malicious actor.  The data could be tampered with in transit which could cause the stored data to be different than the requested data from the customer.
threats.mitigation: Data flowing from infinity to VS service uses https and also requires SAX authentication.

data.name:
Data transmitted to
AI model
data.description: Communication between the VS service and Gateway, https and SAX authentication utilized.  DATA: Customer documents to be vectorized

threats.title: Intercepted data
threats.description: Data in transit could be intercepted and changed.
threats.mitigation: Data flowing from VS to gateway service uses https and also requires SAX authentication.

threats.title: Weak TLS
threats.description: A weak TLS algorithm could allow for communication between the services to be compromised
threats.mitigation: All communication will be using TLS 1.2 or newer

data.name: VS Background to GW
data.description: The VS background service sends data to the gateway service, https and SAX authentication utilized.  The background service is responsible for reindexing improperly indexed data.  Data: Customer data chunks to be reindexed

threats.title: Intercepted data
threats.description: Data in transit could be intercepted and changed.
threats.mitigation: Data flowing from VS background to gateway service uses https and also requires SAX authentication.

threats.title: Weak TLS
threats.description: A weak TLS algorithm could allow for communication between the services to be compromised
threats.mitigation: All communication will be using TLS 1.2 or newer

data.name: GenAI Gateway
data.description: The gateway service communicates with vector store, as such communication could be intercepted and should be considered during a threat model.
data.reasonOutOfScope: Not associated with threats for VS service.  Tool is managed by the same team but out of scope for VS service threat model

data.name: SAX
data.description: SAX authentication is integrated with VS to confirm the identify of requests from Infinity/Autopilot

data.name: DBTools
data.description: DB Tools is an administrative tool used by dev teams and operations

threats.title: Compromised Credentials
threats.description: DB Tools is an administrative tool to troubleshoot database issues.  As such when deployed the tool will have direct access to VS database.  The credentials could be compromised and allow for unauthorized access.
threats.mitigation: This tool is only used in development, or if used in production will only be used during troubleshooting and removed after.  DB tools is not deployed by default in production.  This tool is only used by cloudops.  Standard LDAP verification is required to access the tooling to install and access dbtools

threats.title: New STRIDE threat
threats.description: DB Tools has direct access to the VS database.
threats.mitigation: Rotation of credentials is done by the DBMS team.

data.name: Dev/Ops Teams
data.description: Development and operations teams can interact with the VS database through DBTools
data.reasonOutOfScope: The actions are not controlled by the VS team

threats.title: Compromised User Account
threats.description: The account information of a user who can access tooling may be compromised allowing for unauthorized access
threats.mitigation: All standard LDAP protections are in place to avoid unauthorized access.  Additionally the tooling required to access DB tools is only accessible on a Pega laptop.

data.name:
Smart
Chunking
data.description: The smart chunking service communicates with vector store, as such communication could be intercepted and should be considered during a threat model.  The service is a part of vector store, but is managed in a different repository.
